Plotting dagittyplus models with ggdag

Source: vignettes/ggdag-interop.Rmd
ggdag-interop.Rmd

dagittyplus keeps the exported function names and the dagitty S3 class of the upstream dagitty package unchanged. As a result, models you build with dagittyplus are ordinary dagitty objects, and the ggdag package (Barrett), which is built on top of dagitty, can tidy and plot them with ggplot2 without any conversion.

Build a model the usual way:

library(dagittyplus)

g <- dagitty("dag {
  x [exposure]
  y [outcome]
  z -> x
  z -> y
  x -> y
}")

class(g)
#> [1] "dagitty"

The object has class "dagitty", so every ggdag function accepts it directly. The examples below are not run here (so that this vignette does not require ggdag to be installed), but they work as written once you install.packages("ggdag"):

library(ggdag)

# Tidy the model into a tibble of nodes and edges.
tidy_dagitty(g)

# Plot it with ggplot2.
ggdag(g) +
  theme_dag()

# Highlight a minimal sufficient adjustment set for the effect of x on y.
ggdag_adjustment_set(g)

Because both packages share the same dagitty representation, you can move freely between dagittyplus for analysis and ggdag for publication-quality figures.

Developed by Ahmad Sofi-Mahmudi, Johannes Textor, Benito van der Zander, Ankur Ankan.

Site built with pkgdown 2.2.0.
